data.body:
## Completion Verification ‚Äî PASS

Verified every acceptance criterion against the repository. All 5 ACs are met.

---

### AC1 ‚Äî 3 specs exist, each `validate_eval_spec`-clean with required shape

**MET.**

All 3 files present under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`:
- `completion-verifier.eval.yaml`
- `ticket-quality.eval.yaml`
- `spec-alignment.eval.yaml`

Each has:
- `model: anthropic:claude-opus-4-8` (`completion-verifier.eval.yaml:9`, `ticket-quality.eval.yaml:8`, `spec-alignment.eval.yaml:9`)
- `epochs: 3` (all three specs, confirmed)
- `gate: at_least(2)` (all three)
- `coverage_threshold: 0.8` (all three)
- Non-empty `gold_set` (4 entries each)

`test_new_spec_loads_and_is_strict_clean` in `tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py:17‚Äì20` parametrically calls `validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)` and asserts `[]` for all 3.

---

### AC2 ‚Äî Deterministic scorers all registered; judge is disciplined + `gates: false`

**MET.**

All deterministic scorer names used across the 3 specs are present in `REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py:203‚Äì224`:

| Spec | Scorer names |
|---|---|
| completion-verifier | `emits_valid_verdict`, `recall_on_incomplete`, `no_false_fail_on_complete` |
| ticket-quality | `emits_valid_review_result`, `recall_on_seeded_defects`, `no_fire_on_good_cases` |
| spec-alignment | `emits_valid_review_result`, `recall_on_gaps_and_conflicts`, `no_fire_on_aligned` |

Each spec's single `llm-judge` scorer uses `openai:gpt-4o-2024-08-06` (cross-family to `anthropic:`), `temperature: 0`, `seed: 7`, `snapshot: "2024-08-06"`, and `gates: false` (confirmed in all three YAML files).

`test_new_spec_scorers_are_registered_and_disciplined` in `tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py:23‚Äì35` checks all three invariants parametrically.

---

### AC3 ‚Äî Datasets balanced, corpus-tagged, seeded from real cases

**MET.**

Every dataset case in all 3 specs carries a `corpus:` tag (search across `eval_specs/` returned `corpus:` hits for every case). Balances:
- `completion-verifier`: 3 √ó `expect: fail` + 3 √ó `expect: pass`
- `ticket-quality`: 3 √ó `expect: finding` + 3 √ó `expect: pass`
- `spec-alignment`: 3 √ó `expect: finding` + 3 √ó `expect: pass`

Fixture-repo state is carried in a `note:` metadata field, not injected into `ticket_context`/`spec` fields (no answer leakage). Cases are drawn from rebar/snap/dso corpora with recognizable real-world patterns (coverage gaps, dependency cycles, contradictory specs, etc.).

---

### AC4 ‚Äî Offline unit tests assert per-spec: validity, non-empty gold_set, dataset balance, registered scorers

**MET.**

`tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py` (45 lines, 9 parametrized test cases = 3 specs √ó 3 test functions):
- `test_new_spec_loads_and_is_strict_clean` ‚Äî validity via `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`
- `test_new_spec_scorers_are_registered_and_disciplined` ‚Äî registered names + `gates: false` + `validate_scorer`
- `test_new_spec_dataset_balanced_and_gold_present` ‚Äî non-empty `gold_set` + FIRE/NOFIRE balance check

All 4 per-spec dimensions covered.

---

### AC5 ‚Äî `eval-discipline` CI validates all 7 specs at zero API cost

**MET.**

The `eval-discipline` job in `.github/workflows/eval.yml:26‚Äì53` includes a "Validate every packaged eval spec" step (lines 41‚Äì53) that uses:
```python
specs = list(Path("src/rebar/llm/eval_specs").glob("*.eval.yaml"))
for p in specs:
    errs = ev.validate_eval_spec(yaml.safe_load(p.read_text()))
    assert not errs
```
This glob picks up all 7 specs (the pre-existing 4 plus the new 3) at zero API cost. The per-spec offline unit tests (`test_new_eval_specs.py`) run under the main `test.yml` workflow (`pytest -m "not integration and not external"`).

---

**Verdict: PASS** ‚Äî all 5 acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.
